hints.me: 'me' is a pronoun — the direct object
hints.Deus: 'Deus' is a noun meaning 'God' — it's a vocative here
hints.meus: 'meus' is a possessive adjective — it doesn't appear in this sentence

hints.Indicative: Indicative present would be 'iudicat' — 'Iudica' is a command
hints.Subjunctive: Subjunctive present would be 'iudicet' — 'Iudica' is a direct command
hints.Infinitive: The infinitive would be 'iudicare' — 'Iudica' is a 2nd person singular command

hints.dico, dicere, dixi, dictum: These are the principal parts of 'dicere' (to say), a 3rd conjugation verb
hints.indico, indicare, indicavi, indicatum: These are the principal parts of 'indicare' (to indicate/point out)
hints.educo, educare, educavi, educatum: These are the principal parts of 'educare' (to educate)

hints.Nominative: Nominative of the 1st person pronoun is 'ego'
hints.Dative: Dative of the 1st person pronoun is 'mihi'
hints.Ablative: 'me' can be ablative, but here it's the direct object of 'Iudica'

hints.Nominative: 'Deus' looks nominative, but the speaker is addressing God directly — that's vocative

hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied nominatives — 'Deus' follows 2nd declension -us pattern
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us but have genitive -us (e.g. 'manus, manus') — 'Dei' is 2nd declension
